name: Bellows Beach
tagline: On a military base, open weekends only — the trade-off is some of the most untouched white sand on the island.
parking: Free lot inside the base gate
bestTime: Saturday morning for the full day; arrive by 9am for best spot
talkStory: Bellows sits on an active military base that was one of the first sites hit during the 1941 Pearl Harbor attack — a Japanese midget submarine washed ashore here and you can see a marker for it. The weekend-only access keeps it pristine, and the ironwood tree canopy behind the sand gives rare natural shade. Locals camp here with permits and bodyboard the gentle shore break; it is one of Oahu's safest beginner wave spots.
